items.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to exec approval argPattern matching
items.desc:
## Problem

`matchArgPattern` in `src/infra/exec-command-resolution.ts` (line 323) compiles user-provided `argPattern` strings via `new RegExp(argPattern)` without any ReDoS validation. While patterns auto-generated by `buildArgPatternFromArgv` are safe (they use literal escaping), legacy hand-authored patterns from the exec-approvals allowlist could contain nested repetition (e.g., `(a+)+$`) that causes catastrophic backtracking.

## Fix

Add a `hasNestedRepetition(argPattern)` guard before `ne...

items.title: fix(security): cap state directory writes to 0700
items.desc:
## Summary

`writeTextAtomic` computes `dirMode` as `0o777 & ~process.umask()`, which yields `0o775` under the common `umask 0o002`. When the atomic replace helper ensures the parent directory exists, it sets that mode, relaxing a previously hardened `~/.openclaw` from `0700` back to `0775`.

The same applies to `writeJson`, which re-exports from `@openclaw/fs-safe/json` without overriding `dirMode`.

This fix:
- Defaults `dirMode` to `0o700` in `writeTextAtomic` (was `0o777 & ~process.umask()`)...

items.title: security fix(gateway): defend pre-auth device-signature verify against CPU DoS
items.desc:

Bumps three transitively-pinned libraries to their lowest CVE-clean versions. Trivy scans of images built with `aider-chat==0.86.2` reported 7 HIGH-severity CVEs across these three libraries.

- `gitpython` 3.1.46 → 3.1.49 (CVE-2026-42215, -42284, -44243, -44244)
- `pillow` 12.1.1 → 12.2.0 (CVE-2026-42311)
- `urllib3` 2.6.3 → 2.7.0 (CVE-2026-44431)

Closes #5139
Closes #5140

items.llm_title: Aider 配置文件自动执行恶意Shell命令
items.llm_summary: 该条目揭示了一个供应链攻击场景：攻击者可以在恶意代码仓库中预先配置`.aider.conf.yml`文件，利用Aider的默认`auto-lint`设置，诱导用户在执行代码变更时自动运行攻击者指定的任意Shell命令，且用户未收到任何警告。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 攻击者创建一个包含恶意`.aider.conf.yml`的代码仓库，在该文件中配置恶意的`lint-cmd`（例如 `rm -rf /`）。
2. 受害者使用Aider克隆或打开此仓库，并进行任何代码修改以触发自动lint。
3. 配置的恶意Shell命令将在受害者的环境中被自动执行。

items.title: feat(security): gate /restart and /build bot commands behind owner role check
items.desc:
## Problem

`/restart` and `/build` are Telegram bot commands that call `launchctl kickstart -k` to restart the host process. The only access control is `isMain === true` (command must come from the main group) — there is no user-level check. Any Telegram user who can post to the main group can restart NanoClaw.

Discovered during investigation of three unexpected restarts on 2026-05-07 (13:37, 13:52, 14:45).

Relevant code: `src/channels/telegram.ts:96–120`, `src/bot-commands.ts:31–62`.

## Fix...

items.title: [security] fix(webhook): cap request bodies before adapter dispatch
items.desc:
## Summary

This PR caps request bodies accepted by NanoClaw's shared webhook server before adapter dispatch. Webhook adapters are often exposed on a public URL, but the host previously buffered the entire request body into memory before any adapter-level signature or token validation could run.

items.title: fix(security): harden channel installer remote trust boundary
items.desc:
## The problem

When a user runs a channel installer like `setup/install-discord.sh`, it fetches adapter code from a git branch and copies it into the project. That code gets built and runs as the NanoClaw service.

The `add-*.sh` scripts already use a trusted remote resolver (`setup/lib/channels-remote.sh`) to make sure they only fetch from `qwibitai/nanoclaw`. But the `install-*.sh` scripts bypass the resolver completely and hardcode `git fetch origin channels`.

If the `origin` remote points ...

## Failure scenario

1. Create temp `workspace/` and temp `extra/`.

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`

The architectural problem is independent of the crypto: **the signing key lives in the IronClaw host process and signs silently on every invocation once a tool's capability is approved at install time.**

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal-security CLI written in Rust that analyzes shell commands for content-level threats that regex deny-lists do not reliably catch:

# AgenticMail MCP HTTP authorization bypass

## Summary

`@agenticmail/mcp` exposes a Streamable HTTP transport when started with
`--http` or `MCP_HTTP=1`. In that mode, the `/mcp` endpoint accepts requests
without any HTTP authentication layer. A remote client can initialize a
session and call tools directly.

The problem is that the MCP server also exposes tools documented as requiring
`AGENTICMAIL_MASTER_KEY`, and the server process forwards those calls using its
own configured master key. As...
